title: iberis procumbens subsp. microcarpa
intro: Iberis procumbens subsp. microcarpa, also known as dwarf or rock candytuft, is a low-growing, flowering perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Iberis procumbens subsp. microcarpa include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and calm digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to be used for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
